title: Router Recovery Profile
description: Schema v0.1 for router/network device recovery profiles

hardware_version.description: Hardware revision this profile applies to. Use null only when evidence shows the profile is hardware-version independent; use 'unknown' when sources do not identify hardware version.

firmware_version.description: Specific firmware version this profile applies to. Use null only when applies_to_all_firmware_versions is true; use 'unknown' when sources do not identify firmware version.

applies_to_all_firmware_versions.description: True when evidence shows the profile applies across firmware versions, false when firmware-specific, null when unknown.

device_type.description: Type of device (router, switch, access_point, firewall, etc.)

recovery_family.description: Family of recovery mechanisms this device uses (from enums.md)

recovery_methods.description: List of supported recovery methods (from enums.md)

recovery_details.description: Detailed information for each recovery method

timeout.description: Time window for recovery activation in seconds

notes.description: Additional notes about this recovery method

source_evidence.description: Structured evidence supporting recovery claims. Each claimed recovery method should have at least one supporting evidence entry.

source_type.description: Type of source this evidence comes from (from enums.md)

source_document.description: Path to local source document under sources/ if available

supports_fields.description: Profile fields or recovery methods supported by this evidence entry

snippet.description: Short excerpt or faithful evidence summary. Avoid long copyrighted excerpts.

notes.description: Reviewer or collector notes about this evidence

service.description: Service this credential applies to (web, ssh, telnet, etc.)

boot_interrupt_string.description: String/key combination to interrupt boot process

recovery_command.description: Command to initiate recovery from UART prompt

observation_only.description: True when this group is based on observed tested-unit behavior rather than vendor-documented general applicability

passive_tftp_from_router.description: Router acts as TFTP server and the user connects with a TFTP client. Null means unknown or not evidenced, not inferred.

active_tftp_to_router.description: Router acts as TFTP client and the user runs a TFTP server. Null means unknown or not evidenced, not inferred.

default_ip.description: Default IP address of device during recovery mode

default_subnet.description: Default subnet mask during recovery mode

client_static_ip.description: Static client IP address required on the user's computer during recovery, if explicitly evidenced

client_static_cidr.description: Static client CIDR or prefix length required during recovery, if explicitly evidenced

client_ip_assignment.description: Whether the client should use static IP or DHCP during the recovery upload phase

tftp_filename.description: Expected firmware filename for TFTP recovery

listen_port.description: Port used for recovery (usually 69 for TFTP)

required_lan_port.description: Physical LAN port required for recovery, if explicitly evidenced

rescue_ping_ttl.description: Observed or documented ping TTL while device is in recovery mode

tftp_details.description: TFTP protocol behavior details observed or documented for this recovery profile

observation_only.description: True when this group is based on observed tested-unit behavior rather than vendor-documented general applicability

mode.description: TFTP transfer mode, such as octet

filename_required.description: Whether the router requires an exact firmware filename. Null means unknown.

accepted_filename_examples.description: Observed or documented filenames accepted by the recovery service

server_uses_ephemeral_port.description: Whether the router switches from port 69 to an ephemeral transfer port after WRQ/RRQ. Null means unknown.

ack_source_port.description: Observed source port for TFTP ACK packets, if known

block_rollover_observed.description: Whether block number rollover was observed during a successful transfer

client_behavior_requirement.description: Client-side compatibility requirement, such as following the server ACK source port

default_url.description: Default web UI URL during recovery mode

upload_field_name.description: HTML form field name for firmware upload

expected_firmware_extension.description: Expected file extension for firmware files

observation_only.description: True when this group is based on observed tested-unit behavior rather than vendor-documented general applicability

entry_method.description: Machine-readable recovery entry method, such as reset_hold_power_on

button_name.description: Name of the recovery button (reset, wps, etc.)

press_duration_seconds.description: How long to hold the button during power on

press_duration_seconds_min.description: Minimum observed or documented button hold duration during power-on recovery entry

press_duration_seconds_max.description: Maximum observed or documented button hold duration during power-on recovery entry

led_indicator.description: LED behavior indicating recovery mode is active

observation_only.description: True when this group is based on observed tested-unit behavior rather than vendor-documented general applicability

file_type.description: Required or observed firmware file type or extension

official_format.description: Whether evidence indicates the firmware file is an official vendor format

known_successful_versions.description: Firmware versions observed or documented as successfully recovered

post_upload_behavior.description: Required behavior after firmware upload completes

observation_only.description: True when this group is based on observed tested-unit behavior rather than vendor-documented general applicability

wait_seconds.description: Observed or documented wait time after upload before next action

power_cycle_required.description: Whether a manual power cycle is required after upload. Null means unknown.

dhcp_after_power_cycle.description: Whether the client should return to DHCP after post-upload power cycle

gateway_ip_as_admin_url.description: Whether the DHCP gateway IP should be used as the admin URL after recovery

user_warning_after_upload.description: User-facing warning to show after upload completes

observed_outcomes.description: Observed recovery outcomes from testing or documented reports

observation_only.description: True when this group is based on observed tested-unit behavior rather than vendor-documented general applicability

factory_reset_observed.description: Whether factory-reset-like behavior was observed after recovery

firmware_downgrade_supported.description: Whether firmware downgrade or overwrite recovery was observed

source_type.description: Type of source this information comes from (from enums.md)

source_url.description: URL of the original source (if applicable)

source_document.description: Path to local source document in sources/ directory

confidence_level.description: Confidence level of this profile's accuracy (from enums.md)

submitted_by.description: Name/ID of person who submitted this profile

submitted_date.description: Date when this profile was submitted

reviewed_by.description: Name/ID of person who reviewed this profile

reviewed_date.description: Date when this profile was reviewed

blocking_incidents.description: Incident IDs that currently block profile promotion or App-ready use

observation_only_groups.description: Profile field groups that are observed-only and must not be generalized without additional evidence

notes.description: General notes about this device and recovery process

known_issues.description: Known issues or limitations with this recovery method

risk_warnings.description: User-facing device-specific risks to show before recovery

replaces_profile_id.description: Older profile ID replaced by this profile, if any

replaced_by_profile_id.description: Newer profile ID that supersedes this profile, if any

tool_metadata.description: Tool-derived metadata. Collectors should not manually author these fields.
